seoTitle: Import CSV Data in Power BI: A Clean Import Guide
metaDescription: Learn how to prepare and import CSV data into Power BI cleanly, avoiding common import errors so your reports start with accurate data.
intro: Importing CSV files into Power BI seems simple until formatting issues cause messy reports. This guide walks through preparing your CSV file and importing it cleanly, so you can build accurate visualizations without wrestling with data errors later.

steps.heading: Prepare your CSV file
steps.body: Check your CSV file for consistent formatting, correct headers, and clean data before importing, since issues at this stage carry through into your report.

steps.heading: Import the CSV data into Power BI
steps.body: Use Power BI's data import feature to bring your CSV file in, selecting the correct file and confirming the initial data preview looks accurate.

steps.heading: Ensure a clean import
steps.body: Review the imported data for formatting issues, such as misaligned columns or incorrect data types, and correct them before building visuals.

steps.heading: Follow best practices for future imports
steps.body: Establish a consistent process for preparing and importing CSV files so future data updates go smoothly without repeated cleanup.

steps.heading: Use imports to support broader business reporting
steps.body: Once imported cleanly, your CSV data can be consolidated with other sources, such as sales or feedback data, to build more comprehensive reports.

faq.question: What common CSV formatting issues cause import errors in Power BI?
faq.answer: Inconsistent headers, mixed data types within a column, and encoding issues are common problems that can cause a messy or failed import.

faq.question: Can I import multiple CSV files into one Power BI report?
faq.answer: Yes, Power BI supports importing and combining multiple data sources, including several CSV files, within the same report.

faq.question: Do I need to clean my CSV file before or after importing?
faq.answer: It's generally best to prepare your file beforehand, though Power BI also offers tools to clean and transform data after import.

faq.question: Will updating the original CSV file automatically update my Power BI report?
faq.answer: Not automatically unless you set up a refresh connection; otherwise, you'll need to re-import or refresh the data manually.
